About This Video :

Choosing the best health insurance in India depends on various factors such as coverage, premium rates, network hospitals, customer service, and the specific health needs of an individual or family. Below are some of the most reputable health insurance providers in India, known for their plans, benefits, and overall reputation:

1. Star Health & Allied Insurance
Features: Offers comprehensive health insurance plans, including individual and family floater options, maternity coverage, and pre- and post-hospitalization expenses.
Notable Plans: Star Family Health Optima, Star Health Gain Insurance.
Pros: Wide range of plans, cashless hospital network, strong customer service.
2. Religare Health Insurance (Care Health Insurance)
Features: Offers health insurance with various benefits like lifetime renewability, no upper age limit for entry in some plans, and comprehensive cover.
Notable Plans: Care, Care Freedom, and Care Advantage.
Pros: High sum insured options, great customer service, quick claim settlement.
3. HDFC ERGO Health Insurance
Features: A reliable insurance provider known for its comprehensive health coverage, emergency services, and preventive healthcare coverage.
Notable Plans: Health Suraksha, Critical Illness Health Plan.
Pros: Strong network of hospitals, simple claim process, and flexible premiums.
4. Bajaj Allianz Health Insurance
Features: Offers a variety of plans including health insurance, critical illness cover, and top-up plans. They are known for their claim settlement ratio and extensive hospital network.
Notable Plans: Health Guard, Critical Illness Insurance, Top-up Health Plans.
Pros: Excellent claim settlement ratio, great customer service.
5. Max Bupa Health Insurance
Features: Max Bupa provides affordable health insurance plans that offer cashless treatment across a wide range of hospitals and good customer service.
Notable Plans: Health Companion, Health Recharge.
Pros: No capping on room rent, lifetime renewability, global coverage.
6. New India Assurance
Features: Government-backed and offers reliable health insurance options for individuals and families.
Notable Plans: Mediclaim Policy, Family Floater Mediclaim, Critical Illness Plan.
Pros: Wide network of hospitals, affordable premiums, strong trustworthiness.
7. ICICI Lombard Health Insurance
Features: Offers a wide variety of health insurance policies with features such as cashless hospitalization and coverage for daycare treatments.
Notable Plans: Complete Health Insurance, Health Booster.
Pros: High customer satisfaction, instant claim settlement, and wide hospital network.
8. Aditya Birla Health Insurance
Features: Known for their health management features and discounts for staying healthy, like wellness benefits and fitness tracking.
Notable Plans: Activ Health Plan, Critical Illness Plan.
Pros: Good claim settlement, wellness rewards, and affordable premiums.
9. TATA AIG Health Insurance
Features: Provides coverage for all kinds of medical expenses, including hospitalization, critical illness, and surgeries.
Notable Plans: Medicare Premier Plan, Critical Illness Plan.
Pros: Wide network of hospitals, good claim settlement ratio, trusted brand.
10. SBI Health Insurance
Features: Backed by the State Bank of India, it offers affordable premiums and good coverage options.
Notable Plans: Arogya Premier Policy, Critical Illness Plan.
Pros: Reliable, trusted brand with a vast network of hospitals.
Factors to Consider:
Premium: Choose a plan that fits your budget while still offering the coverage you need.
Coverage: Check the sum insured and the inclusions/exclusions in the policy.
Claim Settlement Ratio: Look for an insurer with a high claim settlement ratio, as it indicates their reliability in processing claims.
Network Hospitals: Ensure the insurance provider has a large network of hospitals, especially if you want cashless hospitalization.
Pre and Post-Hospitalization: Check if the plan covers expenses for both pre and post-hospitalization.
Customer Support: Choose an insurer with good customer service for easy claim processing and support.

comments.text:
BEWARE of NIVA BUPA.....They are the most FRAUDESTER company in Medicla insurance. They will never pay you if your claim amount is high. Recently my mother (68 years) got heart attack and treatment was taken in Fortis Hospital but they rejected my claim asking that once she had Arthritis 22 years ago and they need that Prescription of Doctor as hard copy. I explained that at time she had taken medication only for a month, she got relief and never has taken medication since then, also how can any one keep a Doctors prescription for 22 years, but they were adament on asking the 22 years ago prescription and they rejected my claim of 4.5 lacs on that ground. Infact with each day and my follow up they began to raise new request asking for Diabetes First prescription given by Dr. in 2015 (however diabetes was declared in policy), asking for Dengue prescription once she had in 2009.
I visited their office and found people that they settle big claims 1/100 otherwise they raise query in 99% cases of big amount. If you claim amount is equal or twice the premium amount you are paying then it is okay for them to settle the claim, but if your claim amount is higher or equal to policy limit then even GOD will not save you from NIVA BUPA.
They know that my mother is in elderly age and the claim of 4,50 lacs will be paid in 8 premium amount and that too if every thing goes okay with my mother in future, as such playing on a safer side NIVA BUPA choose the way to escape by raising unrealistic query to my claim.
After a long harrassment by them I asked them to cancel my policy and give my premium balance left in a year ( I paid the premium thrice before for same policy of RS. 65000 every year), but they denied this refund also to me. If any one had any doubt I can present the proof to them. 
So, I decided to expose them on every social platform so that no other person shall get befooled by them.
I will recommend people that if your claim is twice the limit of your premium then it is okay for the company to pay you but if you think that your policy is of 15 lacs and you go to the Hospital thinking you have nothing to pay as your Insurance company will take care of you, then you are COMPLETELY WRONG. You have to pay from your pocket and you will be Debt. So instead of paying these company I wil recommend to save the premium amount to your bankers and get them Fixed deposit and use it when required. In either case you have to suffer then why to suffer because of some third person as you will feel more robbed as you have paid twice once you had paid to insurance company and secondly you have paid to Hospital.
Please BEWARE of NIVA BUPA
